_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, which averages the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice and is indexed to 2015 levels (set at 100), currently stands at 141, reflecting a 41% increase since 2015. This rise indicates a substantial climb in prices over the period, although this increase has been uneven across the basket components and largely driven by recent market developments. The index captures global commodity prices sourced from FRED and IMF data.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the most significant price increase at 99% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 34%, wheat at 19%, and sugar at 13%. These changes are primarily driven by specific weather events and disease outbreaks affecting production, such as citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice crops, and frost and drought affecting arabica coffee regions. These commodities are traded in relatively thin markets, meaning smaller supply shocks can lead to sharp price movements, rather than reflecting broad-based inflation.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket index is largely shaped by a few key weather-sensitive crops experiencing supply pressures. Because these are thinly traded soft commodities exposed to environmental factors, their prices can remain elevated over extended periods due to ongoing production risks and supply constraints.
